findings: Navigation sidebar uses <nav> with aria-label='Patient portal navigation'
Skip-to-content link is the first focusable element on every page
Lab results: abnormal values flagged with BOTH red color AND text label 'Abnormal — above reference range' — never color alone
All data tables provide <caption>, <th scope='row|col'>, and aria-sort attributes
Medication interaction warnings include visible icon with tooltip AND text explanation
Critical alerts use role='alert' for immediate screen reader announcement
Non-critical updates (message arrival, appointment confirmation) use aria-live='polite'
Session timeout modal traps focus and announces remaining time via aria-live region
All form fields have visible <label> elements with error messages linked via aria-describedby
Touch targets at minimum 44x44px; medication list rows at 48px height for comfortable tapping
Focus ring visible (3px, #0284C7) on all interactive elements
gaps: Lab trend chart (sparkline) lacks accessible alternative — should include data table summary
MessageThread attachment previews need descriptive alt text generation

domain_boundaries: domains/patients/ — patient demographics, header, allergies (core identity aggregate)
domains/appointments/ — appointment scheduling, reminders, calendar integration
domains/medications/ — active medications, dosage schedule, refill status, interaction checks
domains/labs/ — lab results, reference ranges, trend analysis, clinical flags
domains/messaging/ — secure patient-provider messaging, attachments, read receipts
domains/visits/ — visit summaries, diagnoses, procedures, follow-up instructions
shared/ui/ — clinical UI primitives (ClinicalFlag, ReferenceRange, MedicationRow)
shared/infra/ — FHIR API client, audit logger, session manager, encryption service
state_management: React Query for server-state: appointments list, lab results, medication list
React Context for patient identity (loaded once on auth, available across routes)
URL routing for section selection (/portal/appointments, /portal/labs, etc.)
Local state for message composition (preserved on navigation via sessionStorage)
Zustand store for unread message count (cross-route badge persistence)

adopted: Semantic color tokens with clinical context (feedback.critical, feedback.warning, feedback.normal)
Typography scale designed for readability at 16px body minimum (clinical data legibility)
Consistent spacing tokens used across appointment cards, medication rows, and lab result panels
AlertBanner variants: critical (non-dismissible, red), warning (dismissible, amber), info (dismissible, blue)
PatientHeader standardizes display of name, MRN, DOB, provider, and allergy warnings across all sections
gaps: LabResultsTable and MedicationList have duplicated sort/filter logic — should extract shared DataTable primitive
No automated token sync between design files and clinical component library
VisitSummaryCard uses custom accordion instead of shared disclosure pattern

observations: Patient identity (name, MRN, allergies) persistent across all portal sections — no repeated lookups
Critical lab results shown via prominent AlertBanner at page top with direct link to affected result
Appointment cards show prep instructions prominently (fasting, medication adjustments)
Medication list grouped by active/discontinued with clear visual separation
MessageThread uses familiar email-like two-panel layout — low learning curve for patients
Session timeout modal provides countdown with 'Extend session' action rather than surprise logout
Visit summaries collapsible by section (diagnoses, procedures, medications) for scannability
concerns: No 'Add to calendar' action on appointment cards — patients must manually add to personal calendar
Lab trend chart shows direction but not magnitude — users can't see how far out of range a value is
Medication refill request buried in detail view rather than surfaced on medication list row action

findings: All PHI (name, DOB, MRN, lab results, messages) transmitted exclusively over TLS 1.3
PHI encrypted at rest using AES-256; database columns individually encrypted
Session timeout enforced: 15-minute idle timeout, 4-hour absolute maximum
Re-authentication required before viewing lab results, sending messages, and downloading records
Audit logs capture every record view, message read, and download with timestamp and user identity
Role-based access enforced server-side: patients see own records only
No PHI in URLs, query parameters, or client-side analytics (verified by code audit)
CSP headers configured to block third-party scripts on all portal pages
gaps: Message attachments scanned for malware but not yet for PHI exfiltration patterns
Downloaded visit summaries are PDF with no digital rights management (can be forwarded without audit)

tests: LabResultDetail: abnormal values render with correct ClinicalFlag variant and aria attributes
AppointmentCard: renders date, provider, location, and prep instructions from FHIR resource
MedicationList: interaction warning icon appears when drug-drug interaction data present
SessionTimeoutModal: countdown decrements, 'Extend' calls refresh endpoint, expiry navigates to login
MessageThread: send message creates FHIR Communication; error state preserves draft text
axe-core accessibility scan passes with zero violations on all portal routes
Visual regression test at 1024px and 375px breakpoints for all portal sections
performance: Portal shell LCP under 2.2s with patient header rendered from cached identity
Lab results table virtualized for patients with 100+ historical results
Medication list renders under 50ms for typical patient (5-15 medications)
Message list paginated at 20 conversations; thread messages paginated at 50 per page
No Cumulative Layout Shift — all card components have stable dimensions during data load
maintainability: Components named by clinical domain (LabResultsTable, MedicationList) not visual style
FHIR types defined in shared/infra/fhir-types.ts — no raw JSON parsing in feature code
Clinical design tokens referenced via CSS custom properties — zero raw hex in feature styles
Each domain module under 400 lines with documented FHIR resource dependencies
